description_long: This is a round, woven basket-like bucket with a pronounced, arching bail handle attached at two points on the rim. The body is constructed from interlaced strips, likely of wood or rattan, forming a lattice pattern that gives it a rustic, hand-crafted appearance. The coloration is a mix of warm browns and darker accents, suggesting natural materials and some weathering or use. The opening is wide and circular, with no visible lid, and the interior appears hollow and spacious. The handle is thin, flexible, and matches the material of the body, looping high above the rim for easy carrying. There are no visible labels, markings, or reinforcements, and the overall form is lightweight and portable, suitable for carrying small loads or gathering items.
description: A round, woven wooden bucket with a high arching handle and a rustic, natural finish. The open top and lattice body suggest it is lightweight and hand-crafted.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide opening, woven lattice body, and the full arch of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the body and the attachment points of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite front view with a clear look at the open top and the symmetry of the woven pattern.
description_view_3: Alternate side view showing the handle's attachment and the rounded profile of the bucket.
